Let me check I understood: I board at five, and I check in again before four thirty.
Who can I talk to about compensation? I'd like to file a complaint tonight.
Next, please. How can I help you tonight?
Good evening. My flight to New York has been canceled, and I have a meeting in Manhattan at nine tomorrow morning. What are my options tonight?
Let me look. The next direct flight is overbooked, I'm afraid, and the one after that is full too. Nothing before tomorrow afternoon.
Tomorrow afternoon is too late for me. If you can get me into JFK before eight in the morning, I'll take any route -- I don't need a direct flight.
All right. Then I can rebook you through Charlotte. You board here at five in the morning, and you land at Kennedy at six ten.
That works. As long as I'm in Manhattan by eight, I'll be fine. And what happens if that flight is late too?

Then we sort it out in Charlotte -- your connection is protected, so if you miss it, we put you on the next one. Here is a meal voucher and a hotel voucher for tonight. The shuttle leaves from door three, every twenty minutes.
Thank you. Sorry, I didn't catch the door number for the shuttle. And one more thing: if I want to request compensation for tonight, where do I file the complaint?
My flight has been canceled. What are my options tonight?
Will I get a meal voucher and a hotel voucher for tonight?
Unless there is a seat tonight, I'll end up sleeping at the airport.
I'd like to file a complaint and request compensation.

Good evening, ladies and gentlemen. This is a gate announcement for flight fourteen eighty-seven to New York Kennedy. I am sorry to tell you that this flight has been canceled tonight because of the weather in the northeast. Please do not leave the gate area yet. If you have a meeting or a connection tomorrow morning, come to the desk and we will rebook you at no extra cost. If you booked through a travel agent, you will need to call your agent directly, because we cannot change that reservation here. Every passenger tonight will receive a meal voucher, and passengers with a long wait will also receive a hotel voucher. Unless you hear your name on this speaker, please stay in the line. We will start with the first twenty passengers, and we will get everyone out of Miami tonight or early tomorrow morning. Thank you for your patience.

Hello, this is Karen from the customer care team, returning your call about flight fourteen eighty-seven. I have good news and one condition. The good news first. I have already rebooked you through Charlotte, and you will land at Kennedy at six ten in the morning. Your seat is confirmed, and you will board here in Miami at five o'clock. Now the condition. You must check in again at the counter before four thirty, because a different airline operates the first part of the trip. If you are not at that counter by four thirty, the system will release your seat, and I cannot promise you anything after that. Your hotel is two minutes from the terminal by shuttle, and your meal voucher is already in your email. If anything changes tonight, call this number again and ask for me. Have a good rest.
